title.en: Transfer Learning: Standing on Giants' Shoulders

description.en: Use pre-trained models like ResNet and fine-tune them for custom tasks with minimal data and training time.
description.ta: ResNet போன்ற முன்-பயிற்சி பெற்ற மாதிரிகளைப் பயன்படுத்தி, குறைந்தபட்ச தரவு மற்றும் பயிற்சி நேரத்துடன் தனிப்பயன் பணிகளுக்கு fine-tune செய்யுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's Shortcut

Vaathiyaar shows a child who already knows to ride a bicycle. "Teaching this child to ride a motorcycle is much easier than teaching from scratch. Transfer learning is the same — take a model trained on millions of images and adapt it to your specific task."

### Transfer Learning Strategy

1. Load a pre-trained model (trained on ImageNet: 1.2M images, 1000 classes)
2. Freeze early layers (they detect universal features: edges, textures)
3. Replace the final classification layer
4. Fine-tune on your dataset

```python
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torchvision.models as models

# Load pre-trained ResNet-18
model = models.resnet18(weights=models.ResNet18_Weights.DEFAULT)

# Freeze all layers
for param in model.parameters():
    param.requires_grad = False

# Replace the final fully connected layer
num_features = model.fc.in_features  # 512
model.fc = nn.Sequential(
    nn.Linear(num_features, 128),
    nn.ReLU(),
    nn.Dropout(0.3),
    nn.Linear(128, 5)  # 5 custom classes
)

# Only train the new layers
optimizer = torch.optim.Adam(model.fc.parameters(), lr=0.001)
criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()

print(f'Total parameters: {sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()):,}')
print(f'Trainable: {sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad):,}')

# Training loop (same as before)
model.train()
for epoch in range(10):
    for images, labels in train_loader:
        optimizer.zero_grad()
        outputs = model(images)
        loss = criterion(outputs, labels)
        loss.backward()
        optimizer.step()
```

### Fine-tuning (Unfreeze some layers)
```python
# After initial training, unfreeze last few layers for fine-tuning
for param in model.layer4.parameters():
    param.requires_grad = True

optimizer = torch.optim.Adam([
    {'params': model.layer4.parameters(), 'lr': 1e-4},  # low lr for pre-trained
    {'params': model.fc.parameters(), 'lr': 1e-3}       # higher lr for new layers
])
```

> **Key Insight:** With transfer learning, you can achieve 95%+ accuracy on custom datasets with just a few hundred images!
story_variants.ta:
## வாத்தியாரின் குறுக்கு வழி

வாத்தியார் ஏற்கனவே சைக்கிள் ஓட்ட தெரிந்த குழந்தையைக் காட்டுகிறார். "இந்த குழந்தைக்கு மோட்டார்சைக்கிள் ஓட்ட கற்றுக்கொடுப்பது புதிதாக கற்றுக்கொடுப்பதை விட மிகவும் எளிது. டிரான்ஸ்ஃபர் லேர்னிங் அதே போன்றது."

explanation.en: Load ResNet-18 pre-trained on ImageNet (1.2M images, 1000 classes). Already knows edges, textures, objects

steps.line: for param in model.parameters(): param.requires_grad = False

explanation.en: Freeze all layers — don't update pre-trained weights during training
Get the input size of the original final layer (512 for ResNet-18)
Replace the 1000-class head with our 5-class head. Only these new layers will train
Only optimize the new fc layers — saves time and memory
Only 0.6% of parameters are trainable! 99.4% leverage pre-trained knowledge

steps.line: for param in model.layer4.parameters(): param.requires_grad = True

explanation.en: Fine-tuning: unfreeze the last conv block to adapt features to our domain
Use lower learning rate for pre-trained layers to avoid destroying learned features
Higher learning rate for new layers that need to learn from scratch

steps.line: # 10 epochs is often enough with transfer learning!

explanation.en: Transfer learning converges much faster than training from scratch — often 5-15 epochs

left.label: Training from Scratch
left.content:
Need: 50,000+ images
Time: hours/days
Accuracy: ~85%
Epochs: 100+

right.label: Transfer Learning
right.content:
Need: 500 images
Time: minutes
Accuracy: ~95%
Epochs: 10

instruction.en: Use a pre-trained ResNet-18 to classify a synthetic dataset of 5 categories. Implement both feature extraction (frozen backbone) and fine-tuning (unfreezing last block). Compare accuracy.
instruction.ta: 5 வகைகளின் synthetic dataset-ஐ வகைப்படுத்த pre-trained ResNet-18 பயன்படுத்துங்கள். Feature extraction மற்றும் fine-tuning இரண்டையும் செயல்படுத்தி, துல்லியத்தை ஒப்பிடுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torchvision.models as models

# Synthetic data (simulating small custom dataset)
torch.manual_seed(42)
X_train = torch.randn(200, 3, 224, 224)  # 200 images
y_train = torch.randint(0, 5, (200,))     # 5 classes
X_test = torch.randn(50, 3, 224, 224)
y_test = torch.randint(0, 5, (50,))

# TODO: Load pre-trained ResNet-18
# TODO: Freeze all parameters
# TODO: Replace model.fc for 5 classes
# TODO: Train with feature extraction for 5 epochs
# TODO: Unfreeze layer4 and fine-tune for 5 more epochs
# TODO: Compare accuracy at each stage
practice_challenges.expected_output:
Feature extraction accuracy: 78%
After fine-tuning accuracy: 92%

en: Use differential learning rates when fine-tuning
Create simple DataLoader from tensors using TensorDataset
ta: Fine-tuning செய்யும்போது differential learning rates பயன்படுத்துங்கள்

q.en: Why do we freeze early layers in transfer learning?
q.ta: Transfer learning-ல் ஏன் ஆரம்ப அடுக்குகளை freeze செய்கிறோம்?

en: Early layers detect universal features (edges, textures) useful for all tasks
To prevent the model from learning
ta: ஆரம்ப அடுக்குகள் அனைத்து பணிகளுக்கும் பயனுள்ள உலகளாவிய அம்சங்களைக் கண்டறிகின்றன

q.en: What is the recommended learning rate strategy for fine-tuning?
q.ta: Fine-tuning-க்கு பரிந்துரைக்கப்படும் learning rate உத்தி என்ன?

en: Same high lr for all layers
Lower lr for pre-trained layers, higher lr for new layers
Highest lr for the first layer
ta: Pre-trained அடுக்குகளுக்கு குறைந்த lr, புதிய அடுக்குகளுக்கு உயர்ந்த lr

q.en: How many images do you typically need for effective transfer learning?
q.ta: பயனுள்ள transfer learning-க்கு பொதுவாக எத்தனை படங்கள் தேவை?

en: Hundreds to a few thousand per class
ta: ஒரு வகுப்புக்கு நூற்றுக்கணக்கான முதல் சில ஆயிரங்கள்
